sections.heading: What is this project?
sections.body: This is a full-stack MERN portfolio & personal website starter. It is built with MongoDB, Express.js, React, and Node.js. The project is designed for developers, network engineers, and freelancers who need a professional online presence with a powerful admin panel.

sections.heading: Tech Stack
sections.body:
Frontend: React 18, React Router v6, Tailwind CSS, Axios, Firebase Auth
Backend: Node.js, Express.js, MongoDB (Atlas), Mongoose, JWT, Multer, Firebase Admin
Storage: ImgBB API for image uploads, Firebase Storage
Deployment: Can be deployed on Vercel (frontend) + Render/Railway (backend) + MongoDB Atlas

sections.heading: Key Features
sections.body:
- Dynamic Hero, About, Services, Works, Coding Reference, Networking, Gallery, Blog, Contact sections
- Full admin panel with CRUD for every section
- Firebase Google login support
- Visitor tracking with analytics
- Commands sidebar panel (CmdsPanel) for quick code reference
- JWT-based authentication
- Responsive design тАФ mobile, tablet, desktop
- Seed data for instant setup

sections.heading: Root Level
sections.body:
mern-stack-starter-temp/
тФЬтФАтФА backend/          тЖР Node.js + Express API
тФФтФАтФА frontend/         тЖР React application

тФЬтФАтФА middleware/       тЖР authMiddleware.js (JWT protect + adminOnly)
тФЬтФАтФА models/           тЖР Mongoose schemas
тФЬтФАтФА routes/           тЖР Express routers
тФЬтФАтФА uploads/          тЖР Local file storage (images, pdfs)
тФЬтФАтФА utils/            тЖР imgbb.js helper
тФЬтФАтФА .env              тЖР Environment variables
тФЬтФАтФА package.json
тФЬтФАтФА seed.js           тЖР Standalone seed script
тФФтФАтФА server.js         тЖР Entry point

sections.heading: Frontend Structure
sections.body:
frontend/src/
тФЬтФАтФА components/
тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА common/       тЖР ImageUpload, LoadingSpinner, ProtectedRoute, RichTextEditor
тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА layout/       тЖР Navbar, Footer, AdminLayout, HeroLayout, CmdsPanel

тФВ   тФФтФАтФА VisitorCounter.jsx
тФЬтФАтФА context/          тЖР AuthContext.js
тФЬтФАтФА hooks/            тЖР useFirebaseStorage.js, useVisitorTracker.js
тФЬтФАтФА pages/
тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА admin/        тЖР All admin CRUD pages
тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА auth/         тЖР Login, Register

тФЬтФАтФА utils/

тФЬтФАтФА config/

sections.heading: Step 1 тАФ Clone and Install
sections.body:
# Clone the project
git clone <your-repo-url>
cd mern-stack-starter-temp

# Install backend dependencies
cd backend
npm install

# Install frontend dependencies
cd ../frontend
npm install

sections.heading: Step 2 тАФ Environment Variables
sections.body:
Copy .env.example to .env in both backend and frontend folders.

Backend .env:
PORT=5000
MONGODB_URI=mongodb+srv://<user>:<pass>@cluster.mongodb.net/dbname
JWT_SECRET=your_secret_here
JWT_EXPIRE=7d
NODE_ENV=development
IMGBB_API_KEY=your_imgbb_key
FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=your_project_id
FIREBASE_CLIENT_EMAIL=your_client_email
FIREBASE_PRIVATE_KEY="-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----\n...\n-----END PRIVATE KEY-----\n"

Frontend .env:
REACT_APP_API_URL=http://localhost:5000/api
REACT_APP_FIREBASE_API_KEY=...
REACT_APP_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=...
REACT_APP_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=...

sections.heading: Step 3 тАФ Fix Case-Sensitive Filename (Linux)
sections.body:
On Linux, file names are case-sensitive. Run this fix before starting:

mv backend/data/Service.seed.json backend/data/service.seed.json

This is needed because server.js requires './data/service.seed.json' (lowercase s).

sections.heading: Step 4 тАФ Run the App
sections.body:
Open TWO terminals:

# Terminal 1 тАФ Backend
cd backend
npm run dev

# Terminal 2 тАФ Frontend
cd frontend
npm start

Backend runs on: http://localhost:5000
Frontend runs on: http://localhost:3000

sections.heading: Step 5 тАФ Seed Data
sections.body:
Seed data runs automatically on first server start. You will see in terminal:
тЬЕ MongoDB connected
тЬЕ Hero seeded.
тЬЕ AboutInfo seeded.
тЬЕ Services seeded.
... and so on.

If you want to re-seed manually:
cd backend
node seed.js

sections.heading: Base URL
sections.body:
Development: http://localhost:5000/api
All admin routes require: Authorization: Bearer <token> header

sections.body:

POST   /api/auth/login       тАФ Login (returns JWT)
POST   /api/auth/firebase    тАФ Firebase Google login
GET    /api/auth/me          тАФ Get current user (protected)
PUT    /api/auth/profile     тАФ Update profile (protected)

sections.heading: Route Protection
sections.body:
ProtectedRoute component (src/components/common/ProtectedRoute.js) wraps all /admin/* routes.
It checks AuthContext for a valid user. If not logged in, redirects to /admin/login.
AdminLayout wraps every admin page with the sidebar navigation.

sections.heading: How to Access
sections.body:
Go to: http://localhost:3000/admin/login
Register once: http://localhost:3000/admin/register
After login you land on the Dashboard.

sections.heading: Sidebar Navigation Items
sections.body:
Dashboard         тАФ Stats overview (blog count, team count, visitors)
Hero Section      тАФ Edit name, tagline, description, profile image, CV link, chips
About Section     тАФ Edit paragraphs, info cards, stats
Clients           тАФ Add/Edit/Delete client logos
Experiences       тАФ Timeline of work/education experiences
Services          тАФ Services cards
Coding Ref        тАФ Code snippet reference cards
Networking        тАФ Network topic cards
Blogs             тАФ Create, edit, publish/draft blog posts
Team              тАФ Team member cards
Works             тАФ Portfolio project cards
Photo Categories  тАФ Gallery category management
Gallery           тАФ Photo upload and management
Contact Info      тАФ Phone, email, address
Footer & Navbar   тАФ Logo, brand name, social links
Documentation     тАФ This page! Manage site docs
Settings          тАФ Profile settings

sections.heading: Image Upload Flow
sections.body:
1. Admin selects an image in any form.
2. ImageUpload component sends it to POST /api/upload/image.
3. Backend (utils/imgbb.js) uploads to ImgBB API.
4. ImgBB returns a public URL.
5. URL is saved to MongoDB.
6. Public site reads the URL directly from MongoDB.

sections.heading: JWT Auth Flow
sections.body:
1. Admin registers at /admin/register (creates user in MongoDB with bcrypt-hashed password).
2. Logs in at POST /api/auth/login тАФ server returns a JWT token (7 days).
3. Frontend stores token in localStorage.
4. Every API request sends: Authorization: Bearer <token>.
5. authMiddleware.js (protect) verifies the token and attaches req.user.
6. adminOnly middleware checks req.user.role === 'admin'.

sections.heading: Firebase Google Login
sections.body:
1. Frontend triggers Google popup via Firebase SDK.
2. Gets Firebase idToken.
3. Sends to POST /api/auth/firebase.
4. Backend verifies with Firebase Admin SDK.
5. Creates or finds user in MongoDB.
6. Returns JWT тАФ same flow as normal login.

sections.heading: What is it?
sections.body: CmdsPanel is a floating sidebar triggered by the CMDS button on the right side of the homepage. It shows quick code/command references for Network, MERN, and Laravel topics.

sections.heading: Files Involved
sections.body:
src/components/CmdsPanel.jsx        тЖР CORRECT version with command cards (use this)
src/components/layout/CmdsPanel.js  тЖР Old simple version (shows 'No recent commands')
src/components/layout/HeroLayout.js тЖР Contains the CMDS toggle button + imports CmdsPanel

sections.heading: Fix: Wrong CmdsPanel Being Used
Adding New Commands
sections.body:
Open src/components/CmdsPanel.jsx.
Add a new object to the commands array:
{
  title: "Your Command Title",
  tag: "NETWORK",
  tagStyle: { background: "rgba(250,204,21,0.08)", color: "#facc15", border: "1px solid rgba(250,204,21,0.25)" },
  code: `your command here`,
}

sections.heading: Frontend тАФ Vercel
sections.body:
1. Push frontend/ folder to GitHub.
2. Import to Vercel.
3. Build command: npm run build
4. Output dir: build
5. Add all REACT_APP_* environment variables in Vercel dashboard.

sections.heading: Backend тАФ Render or Railway
sections.body:
1. Push backend/ folder to GitHub.
2. Import to Render (or Railway).
3. Start command: node server.js
4. Add all backend .env variables in Render dashboard.
5. Get your deployed URL (e.g. https://your-app.onrender.com).

sections.heading: Connect Frontend to Deployed Backend
sections.body:
In Vercel, set:
REACT_APP_API_URL=https://your-backend.onrender.com/api

Also update backend server.js CORS:
app.use(cors({ origin: "https://your-frontend.vercel.app", credentials: true }));

sections.heading: MongoDB Atlas
sections.body:
1. Create a free cluster at mongodb.com/atlas.
2. Create a DB user with read/write access.
3. Whitelist IP: 0.0.0.0/0 (allow all) for cloud deployment.
4. Get the connection string and paste in MONGODB_URI.

sections.heading: ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED on port 5000
sections.body:
Cause: Backend server is not running.
Fix:
cd backend && npm install && npm run dev

Make sure you see: ЁЯЪА Server running on port 5000

sections.heading: MongoDB connection error
sections.body:
Cause: Wrong MONGODB_URI or IP not whitelisted.
Fix:
1. Check backend/.env MONGODB_URI is correct.
2. In MongoDB Atlas тЖТ Network Access тЖТ Add IP 0.0.0.0/0.
3. Verify username and password in the connection string.

sections.heading: service.seed.json not found (Linux)
sections.body:
Cause: File is named Service.seed.json but server.js requires service.seed.json (lowercase).
Fix:
mv backend/data/Service.seed.json backend/data/service.seed.json

sections.heading: CmdsPanel shows 'No recent commands'
Images not uploading
sections.body:
Cause: IMGBB_API_KEY is missing or wrong.
Fix:
1. Get a free API key from imgbb.com.
2. Set IMGBB_API_KEY=your_key in backend/.env.
3. Restart backend server.
